name: Kasha with Mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Soak porcini mushrooms in hot chicken broth.
3. Heat olive oil in a large, nonstick saucepan.
4. Saute onion, garlic, and bell pepper over medium heat until golden, about 5 minutes.
5. Add white mushrooms; increase heat to high. Cook until browned and most of the liquid has evaporated, about 5 minutes.
6. Mix kasha and egg white in a bowl. Add to vegetables; cook over high heat for 2 minutes, or until grains are separate and dry.
7. Stir broth, carrots, seasonings, and soaked porcini mushrooms into kasha; bring to a boil.
8. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until tender and liquid is absorbed (18 minutes), stirring occasionally to avoid sticking.
9. Remove from heat and stir in parsley and soy sauce.
10. Spoon into a large casserole dish. Bake until heated, about 10 minutes. Serves 4. Makes 11 cups.
